famous quote:
"Child,' said the Lion, 'I am telling you your story, not hers. No one is told any story but their own."
-- C.S. Lewis (The Horse and His Boy (Chronicles of Narnia, #5))
Interview Text:
Will:

(part 6)

After hearing how clearly that voice came to him, I asked Will what he does to be able to hear God's voice better.

He said a word from the spirit should be quickening. When you find out what it is, you know what you need to do, so just go do it.

But sometimes the actual hearing of the word is more direct than others. There are times you need to hear God's voice directly, but sometimes you won't. You're going to know who it is when it is direct. You know because it just puts you in the right position spiritually to receive that voice.

But when he speaks in a less direct voice it's a little harder to recognize. It's hard when you're reading a lot or are always in a constant mode of input. Because sometimes you'll get ideas from those sources. But you'll be able to identify those as different because they take you right into conviction and shame. Or they can over-emphasize grace, and you'll lose your sense of responsibility.

But when God speaks to you, it's always in a way that feels right. It's like it's aligning in a way you feel it should be.
"When I heard it, there was no shame," Will said. "Just the blindingly obvious wrongness of what I was going to do. It did not make me doubt my salvation or need to defend myself."
I then asked him how one prepares themselves to receive messages like that? How do we make ourselves open to hearing messages from God?

Will said there are two ways.

First, by seeking and asking for it. Not just asking on the night that you hope to recieve, but over and over as a life habit. Use the Lord's prayer as a model to pray. Ask God how can I be part of your kingdom.

And second, we help to hear God by removing distractions. This is how his tastes began to change. He used to be a political junky, reading all sorts of articles and always listening to talk radio. He also spent a lot of time reading fiction, and doing online gaming. But he literally has no desire to do that stuff anymore. Those things aren't sinful in themselves, but it's a lot of distractions. They just aren't as interesting to Will anymore as what God is doing.
Music Video Instructions: Imagine if God's love for us, and ours for him was like this.
